'''David Klemmer''' (born 22 December 1993) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer, who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for [[St Helens RFC|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]], and [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] international level.

He previously played for the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] and the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the NRL. Klemmer has played for [[City New South Wales rugby league team|City Origin]] and [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in the [[State of Origin series]].

==Background==
Klemmer was born in [[Sydney]], New South Wales, Australia and is of [[Germans|German]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/bulldogs/aidan-tolman-says-the-bulldogs-deserve-to-be-in-the-top-four/news-story/33827c68eb4a0047af05fbca58e135f9 |title=Michael Lichaa says the Bulldogs deserve to be in the top four |publisher=Fox Sports |first=Matt |last=Logue |date=8 August 2016}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Toongabbie Tigers|All Saints Toongabbie Tigers]], before being signed by the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]]. He also played [[Australian rules football]] and played for the [[Sydney Swans]] academy.<ref>{{cite web|author=Adrian Proszenko|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/belmore-behemoth-to-scale-greater-heights-20130418-2i32g.html|title=Belmore behemoth to scale greater heights|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=18 April 2013|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref>

==Playing career==
At 15 years of age, Klemmer signed with the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]].<ref name="dailytelegraph1">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/kg-pup-david-klemmer-ready-to-tear-apart-the-nrl/story-fngiu1n0-1226532448852|title=David Klemmer Ready to Tear Apart the NRL|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref> In 2010 and 2011, Klemmer played for the Australian Schoolboys.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://westfieldsports.nsw.edu.au/SPORTS/rl-124.html|title=Westfields Sports High School, Australia's First Sports High School|publisher=Westfieldsports.nsw.edu.au|date=2011-07-27|access-date=2015-11-23|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160314151106/http://westfieldsports.nsw.edu.au/SPORTS/rl-124.html|archive-date=14 March 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref> In 2011, Klemmer also played for the New South Wales under 18s team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://westfieldsports.nsw.edu.au/SPORTS/rl-123.html|title=Westfields Sports High School, Australia's First Sports High School|publisher=Westfieldsports.nsw.edu.au|date=2011-06-08|access-date=2015-11-23|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319082737/http://westfieldsports.nsw.edu.au/SPORTS/rl-123.html|archive-date=19 March 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref>

He played for Canterbury-Bankstown's [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2011 and 2012, scoring 6 tries in 28 games.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rleague.com//db/player/k/klemmer_david/index.php|title=David Klemmer|access-date=4 February 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150702021857/http://rleague.com/db/player/k/klemmer_david/index.php|archive-date=2 July 2015 }}</ref>

In 2012, Klemmer was named on the interchange bench for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in the inaugural under 20s State of Origin match at [[Penrith Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://stevemascord.com/2012/04/21/gamepage-under-20s-state-of-origin-at-centrebet-stadium/|title=Under 20s State of Origin: NEW SOUTH WALES 18 QUEENSLAND 14 at Centrebet Stadium –|publisher=Stevemascord.com|date=2012-04-21|access-date=2015-11-23|archive-date=4 November 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141104104250/http://stevemascord.com/2012/04/21/gamepage-under-20s-state-of-origin-at-centrebet-stadium/|url-status=dead}}</ref> 
[[File:davidklemmer.jpg|thumb|right|Klemmer playing for the Bulldogs in 2012]]
At the end of 2012, Klemmer was named NYC Player of the Year and also named at starting {{rlp|pr}} in the 2012 NYC Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/toyota-cup-team-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/69388/default.aspx|title=Toyota Cup Team of the Year|publisher=NRL.com|date=21 August 2012|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref><ref name="dailytelegraph1"/>

===2013===
On 5 March 2013, Klemmer re-signed with the Canterbury-Bankstown club on a three-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/david-klemmer-signs-1m-three-year-deal-with-canterbury-bulldogs-before-making-his-nrl-debut/story-e6frexnr-1226591080851|title=David Klemmer Signs Three Year Deal with Bulldogs|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref> In round 1 of the [[2013 NRL season]], Klemmer made his [[NRL]] debut for Canterbury-Bankstown against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] off the interchange bench in Canterbury's 12–24 loss at [[Central Coast Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zimbio.com/pictures/zQ0slePDHMP/NRL+Rd+1+Bulldogs+v+Cowboys/XN7AmVsDmy-/David+Klemmer|title=David Klemmer Pictures – NRL Rd 1 – Bulldogs v Cowboys|publisher=Zimbio|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Alex Rowe|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/rugby-league-match-centre/live-bulldogs-v-cowboys-20130309-2fse8.html|title=Live: Bulldogs v Cowboys|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2013-03-09|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref> In round 3, against the [[Melbourne Storm]] at [[AAMI Park]], Klemmer made headlines after he was at the end of receiving a high flying karate style kick from Melbourne's fullback [[Billy Slater]] to the head in Canterbury's 18–22 loss.<ref>{{cite web|author=Brad Walter and Adrian Proszenko|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/warning-for-slater-who-cant-put-a-foot-wrong-20130322-2gl5n.html|title=Warning for Slater, who can't put a foot wrong|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=2013-03-22|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/live-rolling-coverage-of-melbourne-storm-v-canterbury-bulldogs/story-e6frexnr-1226602706828|title=David Klemmer Ready to Tear Apart the NRL|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-11-23|date=21 March 2013}}</ref> 
[[File:David Klemmer.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Klemmer playing for the NSW Under 20s side in 2013]]
On 20 April 2013, Klemmer was named captain of the New South Wales team in the under 20s State of Origin match, leading the team to a 36–12 victory over Queensland at Penrith Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/new-south-wales-defeat-queensland-36-12-in-under-20s-origin/story-e6frexnr-1226625144326|title=NSW Defeat Queensland 36-12 in Under 20s Origin|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-11-23|date=20 April 2013}}</ref> Klemmer debut season ended early after he sustained a knee injury. Klemmer finished his debut year with him playing 4 matches for [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury-Bankstown]].

===2014===
In round 4 against the Melbourne Storm, Klemmer scored his first NRL career try in the Canterbury club's 40–12 win at [[nib Stadium]] in [[Perth]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.gettyimages.com.au/detail/news-photo/david-klemmer-of-the-bulldogs-celebrates-his-try-during-the-news-photo/481262487|title=NRL Rd 4 – Bulldogs v Storm|publisher=Getty Images|date=2014-03-29|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref> On 26 April 2014, Klemmer was selected to play for City Origin for the [[City vs Country Origin]] match at Apex Oval in [[Dubbo, New South Wales|Dubbo]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/city-origin-team-named/tabid/10874/newsid/77969/default.aspx|title=City Origin team named|publisher=NRL.com|date=2014-04-27|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref> Klemmer played off the interchange bench in the 26-all draw on 4 May 2014.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/city-snatch-stunning-draw/|title=City Snatch Stunning Draw|work=Rugby League Week|date=2014-05-04|access-date=2015-11-23|archive-date=3 November 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141103233241/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/city-snatch-stunning-draw/|url-status=dead}}</ref> On 5 October 2014, in Canterbury's [[2014 NRL Grand Final]] against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], Klemmer played off the bench in the club's 6–30 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydney-rabbitohs-win-2014-nrl-grand-final-defeating-canterbury-bulldogs-306/story-fni3fbgz-1227080755667|title=Rabbitohs Win 2014 Grand Final|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-11-23|date=5 October 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/bulldogs-grand-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/82377/default.aspx|title=Bulldogs Grand Final Player Ratings|publisher=NRL.com|date=2014-10-05|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref> Klemmer finished the Canterbury club's [[2014 NRL season|2014 season]] with him playing 23 matches and scoring a try. Klemmer made his international debut for [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]], playing against [[England national rugby league team|England]] at [[AAMI Park]] in Australia's 16–12 win.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/bulldogs-rookie-david-klemmer-to-make-test-debut-for-kangaroos-against-england-in-four-nations-clash/story-fn488j1c-1227109190464|title=David Klemmer to Make Test Debut for Kangaroos|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-23|date=November 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,11070_3730178,00.html|title=Match Report|access-date=4 February 2016|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141224044042/http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0%2C%2C11070_3730178%2C00.html|archive-date=24 December 2014}}</ref>

===2015===
In round 5 of the [[2015 NRL season]], in the Grand Final re-match against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], Klemmer was sin binned after crowding in on referee [[Gerard Sutton (referee)|Gerard Sutton]] alongside Canterbury captain James Graham and yelling "You're off your f***ing face". The Canterbury club were leading 17–16 at the time, but lost 17–18 at [[Stadium Australia|ANZ Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|author=James MacSmith|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/canterbury-bulldogs/bulldogs-james-graham-and-david-klemmer-face-nrl-judiciary-date-20150404-1mekjc.html|title=Bulldogs James Graham and David Klemmer face NRL judiciary date|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=4 April 2015|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref> After full-time was blown, some members of the Canterbury fan base pelted referee Gerard Sutton and match officials with plastic bottles, causing an investigation to find out and ban the misbehaving members from attending rugby league matches for life.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/rabbitohs-v-bulldogs-late-penalty-seals-controversial-south-sydney-win-at-anz-stadium/story-fni3fbgz-1227290516732?sv=b1fd28fd8a2de87000d5d5be79683d68|title=Late Penalty Seals Controversial South Sydney Win|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-11-23|date=3 April 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/match-officials-injured-avoiding-bottles-thrown-by-bulldogs-fans-after-controversial-loss/story-fn2mcuj6-1227290549466|title=Match Officials Injured Avoiding Bottles Thrown by Bulldogs Fans after Controversial Loss|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Michael Chammas|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/canterbury-bulldogs-fans-facing-life-bans-for-throwing-bottles-at-match-officials-as-adam-reynolds-faces-five-months-in-the-stands-20150403-1medqu.html|title=Canterbury Bulldogs fans facing life bans for throwing bottles at match officials as Adam Reynolds faces five months in the stands|date=9 April 2015|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref> Klemmer was later hit with a three-week suspension for contrary conduct. He returned in round 9, against the [[North Queensland Cowboys]], in Canterbury's 23–16 loss at [[Willows Sports Complex|1300SMILES Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/james-graham-david-klemmer-hit-hard-by-nrl-match-review-committee-after-bulldogs-loss-to-rabbitohs/story-fni3fbgz-1227293129223|title=David Klemmer Hit Hard by BRL Match REview Committee|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/state-of-origin/state-of-origin-2015-aggressive-bulldogs-forward-david-klemmer-made-for-origin/story-fn31yxah-1227296586478|title=David Klemmer Made for Origin|publisher=Foxsports.com.au|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-season-2015-north-queensland-cowboys-five-eighth-michael-morgan-stars-in-tight-win-over-canterbury-bulldogs/story-fnp0lyn6-1227347294306?sv=44c6c98ddf2d2270bfdf555f7c03af79|title=Cowboys Michael Morgan Stars in Tight Win over Canterbury Bulldogs|work=The Courier-Mail|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref> On 3 May, Klemmer played for [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City]], playing at prop in the 34–22 loss to [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales Country]] at [[Wagga Wagga]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/country-overcome-city-in-seesawing-affair/tabid/10874/newsid/85906/default.aspx|title=Country overcome City in seesawing affair|publisher=NRL.com|date=2015-05-03|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref> On 27 May, he was selected to make his State of Origin debut for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] in game 1 against [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]]. He played of the interchange bench in the Blues' 11–10 loss at ANZ Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|author=Adrian Proszenko and Michael Carayannis|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/state-of-origin/state-of-origin-bulldogs-debutants-david-klemmer-and-josh-jackson-made-for-it-20150519-gh5dw5.html|title=State of Origin: Bulldogs debutants David Klemmer and Josh Jackson 'made for it'|date=19 May 2015|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin-2015-game-1-live-coverage-of-nsw-blues-v-qld-maroons-at-anz-stadium/story-fni3fqyo-1227371541697|title=Live Coverage of NSW v Queensland|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2015-11-23}}</ref> In game 2 of the [[2015 State of Origin series]], he again made headlines after a heated exchange with the oldest member of the Queensland team, [[Corey Parker (rugby league)|Corey Parker]]. Parker told Klemmer (the youngest member of the New South Wales team) to "show some respect" and Klemmer replied "get f***d you c**t".<ref>{{cite web|author=Brad Walter|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/state-of-origin/state-of-origin-the-verbal-exchange-that-highlighted-the-nsw-blues-winning-attitude-in-game-2-20150618-ghr1zb.html|title=State of Origin: The verbal exchange that highlighted the NSW Blues' winning attitude in game 2|date=18 June 2015|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=2015-11-24}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/state-of-origin-game-2-nsw-blues-beat-queensland-maroons-2618-to-level-series-20150617-ghqocg.html|title=State of Origin game 2: NSW Blues beat Queensland Maroons 26-18 to level series|date=17 June 2015|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=2015-11-24}}</ref> Klemmer also played in game 3, off the interchange bench, in the Blues' 52–6 record loss at [[Lang Park|Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/rugby-league-match-centre/state-of-origin-2015-live-game-3-nsw-blues-v-qld-maroons-20150708-gi7fb7.html|title=State of Origin 2015 Live: Game 3 NSW Blues v QLD Maroons|date=8 July 2015|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=2015-11-24}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/queensland-clinch-2015-origin-series/tabid/10874/newsid/87758/default.aspx|title=Queensland clinch 2015 Origin series|publisher=NRL.com|date=2015-07-08|access-date=2015-11-24}}</ref> He finished off the 2015 season having played in 21 matches and scoring a try for Canterbury-Bankstown.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/NC0xNjYwLTQtLS0tLS0yMDIyMy0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0=|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-10-28|access-date=2015-11-24}}</ref>

===2016===
On 22 May 2016, Klemmer extended his contract with Canterbury-Bankstown until the end of the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/canterbury-bulldogs/canterbury-bulldogs-resign-nrl-rising-stars-david-klemmer-and-moses-mbye-20160521-gp0rl6.html|title=Canterbury Bulldogs re-sign NRL rising stars David Klemmer and Moses Mbye|date=22 May 2016|work=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref> Klemmer played in all 3 matches for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] off the interchange bench in the Blues 2–1 series loss in the [[2016 State of Origin series]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/state-of-origin/state-of-origin-2016-david-klemmer-reignites-feud-with-corey-parker-20160523-gp1wb8.html|title=State of Origin 2016: David Klemmer reignites feud with Corey Parker|first=Michael|last=Chammas|date=23 May 2016|work=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.theaustralian.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin-2016-david-klemmer-endorses-nsw-revolution/news-story/69f023fc898ac3081879ca61dc2b7aa8|title=Subscribe – theaustralian|website=The Australian}}</ref> After Game 2, Klemmer got into some trouble after the Blues 26–16 loss to Queensland after he punched a wall in frustration in the toilets off the [[Suncorp Stadium]] changing rooms.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin/david-klemmer-has-apologised-for-punching-a-hole-in-the-wall-after-game-two/news-story/72fbf4d45b0432eb68f09542b972f7c9|title=Subscribe – couriermail|website=The Courier-Mail}}</ref> Klemmer finished the [[2016 NRL season]] with him playing in 22 matches and scoring a try for Canterbury-Bankstown. On 24 September 2016, Klemmer played for [[Prime Minister's XIII]] against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]], playing off the interchange bench in the 58–0 win at [[Port Moresby]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/prime-ministers-xiii-smashes-papua-new-guinea-580-in-port-moresby/news-story/4a60a5a5a71753d0107dc726e0e952c1|title=Report, pictures: PM's XIII smashes PNG|date=24 September 2016}}</ref> 
[[File:David Klemmer Kangaroos.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Klemmer playing for the Kangaroos in 2016]]
On 3 October 2016, Klemmer was selected in the 24-man Australian Kangaroos squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/live-mal-meningas-australian-kangaroos-four-nations-squad-announced/news-story/44fa5ad17200081cefa9e8b24f39f6bb|title=Kangaroos team: Four Nations squad announcement, Andrew Fifita, Mal Meninga|date=4 October 2016}}</ref> Klemmer played in all 5 matches of the tournament, including playing off the interchange bench in the Kangaroos 34-8 Four Nations final win against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] at [[Anfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/four-nations/four-nations-final-australia-hammer-new-zealand-348-20161120-gstkra.html|title=Four Nations final: Australia hammer New Zealand 34-8|first=Steve|last=Mascord|date=21 November 2016|work=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref>

===2017===
In February, Klemmer was selected in Canterbury's [[2017 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/2017-downer-nrl-auckland-nines-squads/tabid/10874/newsid/103308/default.aspx|title=2017 Downer NRL Auckland Nines squads|date=3 February 2017}}</ref> On 5 May, he played for Australia in the [[2017 ANZAC Test]] where he started at prop in the 30–12 win at [[Canberra Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/live-australia-vs-new-zealand-anzac-test-in-canberra/news-story/f5b42d8e4765c3040d1f0114dff2a2de|title='The greatest I've ever seen'|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref> In June 2017, it was rumoured that Klemmer requested a release from his contract from Canterbury-Bankstown due to a financial dispute stemming from his decision to split with long-time manager David Riolo.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/david-klemmer-refuses-to-commit-to-bulldogs-beyond-2017-20170604-gwk8db.html|title=David Klemmer refuses to commit to Bulldogs beyond 2017|first=James Buckley and Cameron|last=Mee|date=4 June 2017|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref> Klemmer later commented saying that he never wanted to leave Canterbury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/canterbury-deny-australian-and-nsw-blues-prop-david-klemmer-wants-out-of-club-over-pay-dispute/news-story/6799d709bc8c271005239f6efb1e1465|title=David Klemmer Bulldogs release: Canterbury star denies wanting out of club – Fox Sports|website=foxsports.com.au|date=4 June 2017|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref> For the [[2017 State of Origin series]], Klemmer played in all 3 matches off the interchange bench in the Blues 2–1 series loss.<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin/state-of-origin-2016-blues-wrecking-ball-david-klemmer-fired-up-says-sam-kasiano/news-story/a0d900f72142cae845819b6c2487f566| url-status = dead| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20160622234741/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/state-of-origin/state-of-origin-2016-blues-wrecking-ball-david-klemmer-fired-up-says-sam-kasiano/news-story/a0d900f72142cae845819b6c2487f566| archive-date = 2016-06-22| title = State of Origin 2016: Blues wrecking ball David Klemmer fired up, says Sam Kasiano {{!}} Daily Telegraph}}</ref> In the lead-up to Game 3, Klemmer was set to start at prop by coach [[Laurie Daley]] and with [[Andrew Fifita]] to come off the bench but rumours that Fifita blew up at Daley about the switch and he later reversed that decision and the Blues would later go on to lose the match 22–6.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/opinion/whats-the-buzz-nsw-coach-laurie-daley-backflipped-on-decision-to-dump-andrew-fifita-to-bench/news-story/4d487ecba277eb9d619ce3b6adee5342|title=Dailytelegraph.com.au – Subscribe to The Daily Telegraph for exclusive stories|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/state-of-origin/teams/new-south-wales/nsw-blues-cultural-cleanse-could-end-up-to-five-state-of-origin-careers-and-kickstart-six-more/news-story/54cb53777b82c10c8d5c0278065d45e9|title=State of Origin 2017: Andrew Fifita- David Klemmer switch, drinking session to usher in cultural change – Fox Sports|website=foxsports.com.au|date=18 July 2017|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref> Klemmer was named as the winner of the [[Brad Fittler Medal]] for being the Blues Best Player of the series. Klemmer was the second player to receive the award by playing all 3 matches of the interchange bench.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/bulldogs-and-nsw-origin-star-david-klemmer-wins-brad-fittler-medal/news-story/df0cf272d373f7dee1532fe9f00ed25e|title=Dailytelegraph.com.au – Subscribe to The Daily Telegraph for exclusive stories|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref> Klemmer finished the [[2017 NRL season]] with him playing in 21 matches and scoring 1 try for the Bulldogs. On 3 October 2017, Klemmer was selected in the 24-man Australia Kangaroos [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/kangaroos-world-cup-squad-announced/tabid/10874/newsid/112704/default.aspx|title=Kangaroos World Cup squad announced|date=2 October 2017|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref> Klemmer played in all 6 matches of the tournament, including starting at prop in the World Cup Final match against [[England national rugby league team|England]] in the hard-fought 6–0 victory at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/rugby-league-world-cup-2017-australia-scrape-out-60-victory-in-final-against-wayne-bennettinspired-england-20171202-gzxh7d.html|title=Rugby League World Cup 2017: Australia scrape out 6-0 victory in final against Wayne Bennett-inspired England|first=Adam|last=Pengilly|date=2 December 2017|website=The Sydney Morning Herald|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/australia-v-england-player-ratings-who-starred-who-struggled-in-world-cup-final/news-story/56bbc1a62696d3b7e903321d0efb037f|title=Dailytelegraph.com.au – Subscribe to The Daily Telegraph for exclusive stories|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref>

===2018===
In 2018, Klemmer was selected to play for New South Wales in the [[2018 State of Origin series]] which The Blues emerged victorious winning 2–1.  In game 3, Klemmer was placed on report for an attempted trip on Queensland player Ben Hunt. Klemmer was later fined $1350 but escaped suspension.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/07/12/david-klemmer-fined-for-origin-trip/|title=David Klemmer fined for Origin trip|date=12 July 2018|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref>

Late in the year, Klemmer asked for a release from the final two years of his Canterbury contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/bulldogs/david-klemmers-father-insists-family-issues-are-not-the-reason-for-his-sons-possible-bulldogs-exit/news-story/2a515ed030ff6cbaa808f77fe554ab7e|title=NRL 2018 news, Bulldogs: David Klemmer's father, Knights move – Fox Sports|website=foxsports.com.au|date=12 November 2018|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportsnews.com.au/nrl/klemmer-mad-not-to-join-knights-gillespie/456956|title = Klemmer mad not to join Knights: Gillespie}}</ref> He was granted the release in November, signing a five-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] starting in 2019.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2018/11/26/knights-secure-origin-and-test-enforcer-klemmer/|title=Knights secure Origin and Test enforcer Klemmer|date=26 November 2018|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/11/26/david-klemmer-signs-contract-with-newcastle-knights-until-end-of-2023-nrl-season/|title=David Klemmer signs five-year deal with Newcastle Knights|date=26 November 2018|access-date=26 November 2018}}</ref>

===2019===
On 7 October 2019, Klemmer was named in the Australian side for the upcoming [[2019 Oceania Cup (rugby league)|Oceania Cup]] fixtures.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/10/07/six-new-faces-named-in-kangaroos-squad/ |title=Six new faces named in Kangaroos squad |website=nrl.com |date=7 October 2019 |access-date=5 May 2026}}</ref>

===2020===
Klemmer played 21 games for Newcastle in the [[2020 NRL season]]. He played in Newcastle's first finals game since 2013 which was a 46–20 loss against [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] in the elimination final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/11/29/knights-2020-season-by-the-numbers/|title=2020 season by the numbers|website=nrl.com|date=28 November 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
Klemmer made 21 appearances for Newcastle in the [[2021 NRL season]] as the club qualified for the finals. Klemmer played in Newcastle's elimination final loss to [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMjEyNSwyMTM0LDIxNzksMjIyNC0tNC0tLS0tMjAyMjMtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|website=rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref>

===2022===
In round 17 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Klemmer was sent off in Newcastle's 40–28 loss to [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-07-08/nrl-live-score-updates/101214456/|title=David Klemmer sent off as Newcastle fall to South Sydney|publisher=ABC News}}</ref>
On 2 August, Klemmer was stood down by the Newcastle club for an on-field incident which happened during the clubs round 20 loss to [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs|Canterbury]]. It was alleged that Klemmer refused to be substituted towards the end of the match and verbally abused a Newcastle trainer.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-david-klemmer-newcastle-knights-disciplinary-action-reason-dropped-from-team-video-reaction/news-story/18c3a9de90f2b4e5cbdb4ee1296bd928/|title='No one can deny it': Knights player divide revealed as Klemmer ban exposes glaring issue|website=foxsports.com.au}}</ref>
On 16 November, Klemmer joined the [[Wests Tigers]] on a three-year deal in a player swap with [[Jackson Hastings]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-transfer-centre-fonua-pole-wests-tigers-contract-player-movements-signings/news-story/425ee174e833a58fb65b993de2117ad1/|title=Tigers' fierce response to critics as Klemmer-Hastings swap confirmed: Transfer Centre|website=foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===2023===
Klemmer played in all 24 of the Wests Tigers games for 2023. He was 9th in the competition for post-contact metres<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/stats/players/?competition=111&season=2023&stat=1000112|title=Post Contact Metres|website=nrl.com}}</ref> and 20th for run metres,<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/stats/players/?competition=111&season=2023&stat=1000037|title=Run Metres|website=nrl.com}}</ref> leading the club in both statistics. The Wests Tigers would end up finishing with the [[wooden spoon (award)|wooden spoon]] for a second straight year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-wests-tigers-season-in-review-tigers-brutal-review-tim-sheens-benji-marshall-coach-justin-pascoe-wooden-spoon/news-story/d1ae7fb41d93ea7dc29173a824cac5a1/|title='Circus act' that summed up year from hell... and big call left for Benji: Wests Tigers Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===2024===
In round 4 of the [[2024 NRL season]], Wests Tigers won 17–16 over Parramatta at the [[Western Sydney Stadium]]. Parramatta's [[Clinton Gutherson]] had a penalty conversion after the full-time siren to win the match for Parramatta however he missed the kick with camera footage showing Klemmer doing a [[Bras d'honneur]] at Gutherson after the ball sailed wide of the posts. Klemmer was alleged by some fans online of cheating as he waved his arms and jumped in front of the goalposts as Gutherson moved to kick the ball, a distraction which is against the rules.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://au.sports.yahoo.com/nrl-david-klemmer-caught-dubious-clint-gutherson-moment-wests-tigers-win-203416671.html/|title=David Klemmer caught out by NRL fans for dubious Clint Gutherson moment in Tigers win|website=au.sports.yahoo.com}}</ref>
Klemmer made 20 appearances for the Wests Tigers throughout the season as the club finished with the wooden spoon for a third consecutive season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2024-wests-tigers-v-parramatta-eels-spoon-bowl-lachlan-galvin-reaction-social-media-rugby-league-news/news-story/52d07b10167bea71afbbb846921d969c/|title='Either the best or worst game all year': NRL world 'can't look away' from Spoon Bowl|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
On 13 March, Klemmer had signed a deal with the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] for the rest of the 2025 season. Klemmer made his club debut for St. George Illawarra in round 4 of the [[2025 NRL season]] against Melbourne.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-03-13 |title=Much-needed cavalry arrives for desperate Dragons with veteran prop joining from Tigers |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-david-klemmer-set-to-join-dragons-on-oneyear-deal-wests-tigers-shane-flanagan-mikaele-ravalawa-rugby-league-news/news-story/ac286a57000579029756571257bf334a |access-date=2025-03-13 |website=Fox Sports |language=en}}</ref>
Klemmer played a total of 22 games for St. George Illawarra in the 2025 NRL season as the club finished 15th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-nrl-2025-st-george-illawarra-dragons-vs-penrith-panthers-live-scores-teams-videos-blog-stream-clint-gutherson-nathan-cleary/news-story/375961c2b1e20c10d07384509249dece/|title=Cleary masterclass as epic battle with former Panthers superstar locked in after big win|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

On 31 October it was announced that he had signed for [[St. Helens RFC|St Helens]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=31 Oct 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=St Helens land statement signing as veteran NRL powerhouse joins for 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/st-helens-land-statement-signing-as-veteran-nrl-powerhouse-joins-for-2026}}</ref>

===2026===
On 7 February, Klemmer made his club debut for St Helens in their 98–2 victory over [[Workington Town]] in the third round of the Challenge Cup.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/25834740.paul-rowley-said-st-helens-cup-win-workington/|title=What Paul Rowley said about St Helens' cup win at Workington|website=www.sthelensstar.co.uk}}</ref>
